title: Juniata Valley Council Training Committee
text:
Welcome
We are so glad that you found us! We are here to be a training resource to the Juniata Valley Council Scouters. In these pages, you can find information on:
Further resources are available at the National Scouting America Training website.
The training newsletter will be sent to all registered scouters. Please add juniatavalleycouncil@247scouting.com to your safe senders list so you can receive this and other upcoming events in the Juniata Valley Council.

title: Position Specific Training
text:
Training matters. A trained leader can do it easier and then it becomes more fun.
There is required training for all of those in any kind of leadership positions like Council President, Cubmasters/Scoutmasters, Merit Badge Counselors, Chartering Organization Representatives, Youth leaders of National Youth Leadership Program, and more!

title: Chartered Organization Representative
text:
What does this mean?
The Chartered Organization Representative (COR) is the vital link between a Scouting unit and its sponsoring organization (e.g., church, school, or community group).

title: Chartered Organization Representative
text:
What do I do?
Key responsibilities of the COR include:

Liaison Duties: Acting as the primary communication bridge between the Scouting unit, the chartered organization, and the local Scouting district/council.
Leadership & Support: Serving on the unit “Key 3” team (with the committee chair and unit leader) and appointing the unit committee chair.
Approving Personnel: Reviewing and approving all adult volunteer applications for the unit. This also includes that all volunteers are fully trained for thier position.
Representation: Serving as a voting member of the local council and district committee.
Unit Success: Ensuring the unit maintains high-quality programs, active membership recruitment, and follows Scouting America policies.

title: Chartered Organization Representative
section: Do I need to be registered?
text:
Do I need to be registered?
Yes!

title: Chartered Organization Representative
section: Do I need to be trained?
text:
Do I need to be trained?
Yes!
The online training module take ~45 minutes.
Click HERE to take Chartered Organization Representative Training






Note



If you do not already have a my.scouting account, you will be able to create one.


This training focuses on the following topics:

What is a Charter?
Scouting Units
The COR Position
Standards and Volunteers

Scouting America has compiled more information in the Chartered Organization Rpresentative Guidebook

title: Merit Badge Counselor
text:
Do I need Training?
A merit badge counselor is a responsible adult with knowledge and enthusiasm about a career, hobby or other subject who is willing to share that knowledge and enthusiasm with Scouts.
Yes. Merit Badge Counselors are required to take:

title: Merit Badge Counselor
text:
Safeguarding Youth Training
Merit Badge Counselor Training

title: Merit Badge Counselor
section: Register as a Merit Badge Counselor
text:
Register as a Merit Badge Counselor
Contact your local council office to register as a merit badge counselor.
Find out more about Merit Badge Counselors at Scouting America’s National Website.

title: Hazardous Weather
text:
What: Hazardous Weather Training
When: Every 2 years
Who: required training for some positions
Where: Click here to take Hazardous Weather
Alternatively navigate to training by:

Log in to: https://my.scouting.org/
Click on My Training
Click on ScoutsBSA
Click on Catalog
Click on Programs
Type ‘hazardous weather’ in the search bar

title: Safeguarding Youth Training
text:
When: Required Every 1 year
Who: all BSA new and registered volunteers aged 18+
Where: Click here to take Safeguarding Youth Training
Alternatively navigate to training by:

Log in to: https://my.scouting.org/
Click on My Training
Click on ScoutsBSA
Click on Catalog
Click on Programs
Type ‘safeguarding youth’ in the search bar
